input.command:
gh pr create --reviewer patflynn --title "fix(system): set autoUpgrade operation to boot" --body 'On classic-laddie (NVIDIA RTX 4090, Wayland/Hyprland), the nightly `system.autoUpgrade` runs `nixos-rebuild switch`. When a build includes an NVIDIA driver bump, switching activates it live and reloads the `nvidia_drm` kernel module, which drops the DRM connector out from under the running compositor. Hyprland then SEGVs during the monitor disconnect→headless-output transition (crash chain: `CSession::dispatchUdevEvents` → `CDRMBackend::recheckOutputs` → `SDRMConnector::disconnect` → `CMonitor::onDisconnect` → `enterUnsafeState` → `onConnect` → `sendFrameEventsToWorkspace` → `Desktop::View::getViewsForWorkspace`, null workspace deref). Two crash reports were captured (2026-06-14 02:59 and 2026-06-15 23:04), both at rebuild-activation time.

## Change

Set `system.autoUpgrade.operation = "boot"` in `modules/common/system.nix`. This builds the new generation and sets it as the boot default **without** activating it on the running system, so the nightly upgrade no longer reloads the GPU driver on a live session. The new generation takes effect on the next reboot instead.

This module is imported by all hosts, which is the intended scope.

## Validation

- `nix eval .#nixosConfigurations.classic-laddie.config.system.autoUpgrade.operation` → `"boot"`
- `nix eval .#nixosConfigurations.classic-laddie.config.system.build.toplevel.drvPath` evaluates with no errors

Run: 20260616-0902-112c196e'

content.text:
Done. PR created: https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/571

**Summary of the change** (`modules/common/system.nix`):
- Added `operation = "boot";` to the `system.autoUpgrade` block, with a comment explaining the rationale. Left `enable`/`flake`/`flags`/`dates`/`randomizedDelaySec` and the `systemd.services.nixos-upgrade.serviceConfig` block untouched.

**Validation:**
- `system.autoUpgrade.operation` resolves to `"boot"` for classic-laddie.
- `system.build.toplevel.drvPath` evaluates with no errors.
- `klaus _pre-review` passed with no findings; pre-commit hooks (nixfmt, detect-private-keys) passed.

The nightly upgrade now builds the new generation and sets it as the boot default without activating it live, so an NVIDIA driver bump no longer reloads `nvidia_drm` under the running Hyprland session — the new generation takes effect on next reboot instead.
